title: Golden Globes Final Predictions: Major Wins Across Categories
text: Clayton Davis's predictions for the 83rd Golden Globes ceremony highlight films and performances positioned to make history. The article emphasizes balance between prestige projects and popular appeal, with several nominees set to achieve milestone victories. According to the predictions table, Rhea Seehorn from "Pluribus" is projected as the frontrunner, with potential upsets from Kathy Bates and Britt Lower. The article emphasizes several groundbreaking possibilities: Wagner Moura could become "the first Brazilian man nominated" in the lead actor drama category, Ryan Coogler might become "the first Black winner" of the screenplay category if victorious, and Paul Thomas Anderson could achieve a rare hat trick with directing, best picture, and screenplay wins. Key expected winners include "Hamnet" for Best Drama, "One Battle After Another" for Best Comedy/Musical, and Wagner Moura for Best Actor Drama. The ceremony aired January 11, 2026, hosted by Nikki Glaser.
